post.summary: Creates a BisTrack Quote or  Sales order transaction.
post.description:
Before an quote/order can be processed an Order Number  is required. You can retrieve an order number using api/order/ReservedOrderNumber/{OrderType} endpoint
Payments: 
1. Web Track Store Shift4 Method - Collect Later/Delivered
- In this method, the electronic payment is taken as a Pre-Auth using Shift4 
- The information passed from Web Track Store to BisTrack includes:
- The Token from Shift4
- The Merchant ID used for the Shift4 Pre-Auth
- When the goods are delivered/collected in BisTrack, we send an Auth transaction to Shift4 using the Token and the Merchant ID
- BisTrack is responsible for doing the final authorization with Shift4
2. 3rd Party Web Store Method
- In this method, we receive payment details along with the imported order, but it is just really the 
    amount that was paid that we are being told about
- The incoming payments are treated as non-integrated electronic payments
- BisTrack does not authorize the transactions, does not need a token and does not need a merchant ID
- In this case, we are just taking the word of the 3rd party, that a payment was taken for a specific amount

AccountAgingTable.description:
Customer Account aging details -- should always contain 7 entries.  Meanings are determined 
by whether bisTrack is running in AR or GP mode.  See "FinancialDetailsType" property.

When bisTrack is running in AR mode, this table contains seven entries to be displayed 
horizontally in a table showing:

    Overdue:
    No. of Transactions:
    Value of Transactions:
    % of Transactions:

The values of each AccountAgingEntry are mapped into the table from the properties.
Entries for the "Overdue:" row are hard-coded and are as follows:

  Index         Overdue
    0           "Current"
    1           "1 - 30"
    2           "31 - 60"
    3           "61 - 90"
    4           "91 - 120"
    5           "121 - 150"
    6           "151+"


For example:

                Overdue:    Current   1 - 30    31 - 60    61 - 90   91 - 120  121 - 150  151 +
    No. of Transactions:    4         4         4          4         2         0          21
  Value of Transactions:    3,854.48  1,229.84  50,555.28  394.27    312.84    0.00       12,598.68
      % of Transactions:    10%       10%       10%        10%       5%        0%         54%




When running in GP mode, a vertical table is displayed showing only the value of transactions in each grouping:

         Current Month:     Balance0
               1 to 30:     Balance1
              31 to 60:     Balance2
              61 to 90:     Balance3
             91 to 120:     Balance4
                  120+:     Balance5
    Unapplied Payments:     Balance6

Address of the site where e-commerce transaction is originated from.
- It should be a URL.
- It is the mandatory field for E-Commerce transaction.
- It is recommended to only pass the domain, however optionally a path can also be included. Example: consumingapp.com or consumingapp.com/ecommerce
- It can allow maximum length of 13 bytes above that will be truncated.
- It is recommended not to include https and http in ecomm_url field if included will be skipped.
